title: FULL-DUPLEX PASS: caller barges in mid-sentence, agent yields the floor fast (overlap, then a clean yield)

transcript.agent: Your appointment window is Thursday between nine and eleven and the technician will bring the replacement part so the whole...
transcript.caller: hold on, Thursday does not work for me, I need an afternoon slot, what do you have after two

why_it_matters: Both voices are live at once from 2.00 s to 2.60 s: the caller takes the floor mid-sentence while the agent is still speaking, and the agent drops its turn inside the yield and talk-over bounds. This is the correct full-duplex behavior in miniature: overlap itself is not the failure, holding the floor through it is. The event PASSES because the measured floor transfer stays inside both declared bounds. Its bad twin is fdx-02, the byte-identical barge-in where the agent keeps transmitting instead; the pair isolates the one variable that separates a clean yield from a sustained talk-over.
